rulings.Case Title: JUAN ESCOBAR VS. MINISTRY OF TASTE, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel All Defendants To Provide Responses To Discovery Requests; For An Order That All Objections Are Deemed Waived; And For An Award Of Monetary Sanctions Against All Defendants And Attorney
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Thursday, May 20, 2021, Line 3, PLAINTIFF JUAN ESCOBAR's Motion To Compel All Defendants To Provide Responses To Discovery Requests; For An Order That All Objections Are Deemed Waived; And For An Award Of Monetary Sanctions Against All Defendants And Attorney. Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to Compel Defendants to Respond to Discovery without Objections and Sanction - Parties to appear. Parties to deliver hard copies of their papers to the judge pro tem at Law Offices of Robert F. Kane 870 Market Street, Suite 1128, San Francisco, by 7:00 p.m. today, May 19th. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link (DISCOVERY, DEPT 301 @ 9:00AM), or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rkane1089@aol.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If parties have arranged for a court reporter to appear, please include the following in your email: (1) ensure that they are California Certified Shorthand Reporters; (2) the reporter's full contact information [name, CSR number, personal work email, and phone number]. If the reporter is not a California Certified Shorthand Reporter, the transcript cannot be certified for use in California courts. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: FENGMEI LI VS. JAEWOONG CHOI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Requiring Plaintiff To Post An Undertaking With Court Reporter Fee
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 20, 2021, Line 4, DEFENDANT JAEWOONG CHOI, D.D.S.' Motion Requiring Plaintiff To Post An Undertaking: Defendant's motion to require plaintiff to post an undertaking is denied. Code of Civil Procedure section 1030 provides that: "When the plaintiff in an action ... resides out of the state, ... the defendant may at any time apply to the court by noticed motion for an order requiring the plaintiff to file an undertaking to secure an award of costs and attorney's fees which may be awarded in the action ....[the] motion shall be made on the grounds that the plaintiff resides out of the state ... and that there is a reasonable possibility that the moving defendant will obtain judgment in the action ...." Defendant must make a sufficient evidentiary showing that he has a "reasonable possibility" of prevailing. (See Baltayan v. Estate of Getemyan (2001) 90 Cal.App.4th 1427, 1432 [defendant maintained evidentiary burden by citing the arbitration award, the arbitrator's letter, and the police report].) In this case, defendant relies on an expert declaration that the Court previously rejected as incompetent evidence because it was conclusory and the expert did not provide a reasoned explanation. Defendant does not meet his evidentiary burden by relying on the same incompetent evidence. In other words, the declaration of Dr. Gluskin is a nullity. It is true that at trial plaintiff will need to present expert evidence to support her claims. (See Bushling v. Fremont Medical Center (2004) 117 Cal.App.4th 493, 509 [in a medical malpractice case "where the conduct required of a medical professional is not within the common knowledge of laymen, a plaintiff must present expert witness testimony to prove a breach of the standard of care."].) That, however, is her trial burden and is unrelated to defendant's evidentiary burden on the instant motion. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: ELIZABETH BLAND VS. YELP, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Approval Of Private Attorneys General Act Settlement; ***Court Reporter Fees Paid***
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 20, 2021, Line 9, PLAINTIFF ELIZABETH BLAND's Motion For Approval Of Private Attorneys General Act Settlement: Hearing required. Counsel are requested to appear to address the following issues: 1. The anticipated effect of the proposed settlement on the pending action in Low v. Yelp Inc., No. CGC-20-582797. (Silver Decl. 24.) In particular, is the overlapping claim for violation of Labor Code section 1174 in that action asserted on behalf of the same group of aggrieved employees and for the same time period? 2. Whether the plaintiffs/aggrieved employees in the Low action were notified of the proposed settlement and, if not, whether such notice should be provided to allow them to intervene or object. 3. Whether the proposed award of $200,000 in attorneys' fees (33 1/3 % of the gross settlement amount) should be approved in light of counsel's lodestar ($110,680). As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: THE STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA VS. LEGALMATCH.COM, A CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One; Request For Entry Of $10,000 In Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant And Cross-Claimant Legalmatch.Com
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 20, 2021, Line 10, 3. PLAINTIFF THE STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One; Request For Entry Of $10,000 In Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant And Cross-Claimant Legalmatch.Com: The report and recommendation of the judge pro tem is adopted. Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to request for production of documents is granted. Plaintiff's request for sanctions against LegalMatch.com is granted in the amount of $10,000. The Court adds that independent of the State Bar's discovery request, LegalMatch is required by the State Bar's rules to "maintain and provide to the State Bar upon request current records" for each panel member that include "(a) name, contact information, and qualifications; (b) number and type of referrals, including whether referrals were made using purely technological means; and (c) fees remitted for membership, referrals or consultations, advertising; or any other reason," as well as client and other information for each referral. (Rule 3.827(A).) Further, LegalMatch's attempt to resist discovery by asserting the privacy interests of its attorney panel members rings hollow in light of that rule, which provides, "Any record in the possession of the State Bar pertaining to a lawyer referral service is the property of the State Bar and confidential unless authorized for disclosure by these rules, order of the Board of Trustees, or consent of the lawyer referral service." (Rule 3.827(B).) As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the State Bar is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: DIRK NEYHART VS. PETER BRESLER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Answers To Interrogatories And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Thursday, May 20, 2021, Line 1, DEFENDANT PETER BRESLER's Motion To Compel Further Answers To Interrogatories And For Sanctions (Part 1 of 2 for purposes of entry of Tentative Ruling). Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's Motion to Compel Further Answers to Interrogatories and Sanctions - Parties to meet and confer prior to the hearing to discuss what is the "incident" and to appear. With respect to the discovery at issue the court makes the following tentatives as to the form interrogatories. The motion is denied as to form interrogatories 2.3, 2.6, 2.12, 4.1, 4.2, 6.1, 7.2-7.3, 8.1-8.8, and 14.1-14.2. Plaintiff to respond to all parts of form interrogatory 9.1-9.2 based on the incident being the alleged malpractice in failing to find the will. With respect to interrogatory 10.1-10.2 I assume the answer is no rather than not applicable. With respect to interrogatory 12.1-12.3, 12.6. Denied as to Interrogatory 17.1(4), (51), (62), (73), (81), (83), (84), (96), (97), (102), (104), (108), (109), (110), (111), (112), (120), (125), (153), (154), (165), (166), (173), (185), and (195). Granted as to Interrogatory 17.1: (40), (52), (53), (55), (56), (57), (59), (60), (64), (75), (76), (85), (87), (99), (113), (119), (140), (141), (142), (146), (155), (156), (161), (162), and (204), as the defendant is entitled to know the facts supporting your legal position. With respect to 17.1(60) Plaintiff needs to address factual basis of why Neyhart was not a fiduciary. Assuming the following requests were withdrawn, (135), (136), (137), (139) as claimed by Plaintiff, motion to compel is denied. Plaintiff to provide complete Code compliant answers to 17.1(147), (148), (149), (150) as it is not clear what the consideration was. With respect to interrogatory 50.2 and 50.6, it is not clear whether there is an agreement alleged. Code compliant responses to be provided within 30 days of the filing of this order. (Tentative Ruling continues in Part 2 of 2) =(302/JPT)
